findings.note: ast.literal_eval only parses literals, no code-execution path (correct choice vs pickle)
open redirect: next_url from query string passed straight to redirect() with no allowlist/relative-path check
pickle.loads on client-supplied cookie bytes enables arbitrary code execution
unfilled placeholder ('Upload Folder Path]') is an invalid identifier causing SyntaxError before the unsanitized-filename save path would run
SQL injection: phrase interpolated via f-string directly into a LIKE clause
broken auth: unconditionally returns True regardless of any password check, and stores the password in plaintext
yaml.load() with no Loader argument permits arbitrary object construction/code execution via crafted YAML tags
compares password directly against the DB value, implying plaintext storage (also timing-unsafe ==)
SQL injection: name interpolated via f-string directly into a LIKE clause
SSRF: fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no host/IP allowlist and no timeout
yaml.load() with no Loader argument permits arbitrary object construction/code execution
extension check compares a dot-less suffix against dotted strings so it rejects every upload (non-functional); no unsanitized save is ever reached
subprocess.run(command, shell=True) on raw user input is direct command injection
SSRF: fetches arbitrary user-supplied URL, no timeout or destination validation
pickle.loads on client-supplied cookie bytes enables arbitrary code execution (duplicate of DL00428)
SQL injection: query, and unvalidated page_size/offset, interpolated via f-string into raw SQL
computes a proper salted PBKDF2 hash then discards it, returning an unsalted single-round SHA-256 hash instead
open redirect: next param used unchecked in redirect()
compares password directly against the DB value, implying plaintext storage (also timing-unsafe ==)
yaml.load() with no Loader argument permits arbitrary object construction/code execution
built-in hash() has no .hexdigest(), so filename construction raises AttributeError before f.save() runs (upload path non-functional); the serving route uses send_from_directory safely
shutil.unpackzip doesn't exist; AttributeError is caught and returns 500, so extraction never actually happens (no zip-slip currently exploitable)
uses parameterized '?' placeholders (safe) despite a typo ('WHERELOWER') that would raise a SQL syntax error
empty code block, nothing to assess
SSRF: fetches arbitrary user-supplied URL, no timeout or destination validation
pickle.loads directly on raw request data with no JWT validation at all; arbitrary code execution
os.popen(command) on raw user input is direct command injection
parameterized query with '?' placeholders, no injection
unsalted single-round SHA-256 chosen as the password hash (weak vs brute force/rainbow tables); also NameError on undefined 'username'
unsalted single-round SHA-256 computed as the 'secure' password hash, though the stub never actually persists it
base64+json.loads deserialization has no code-execution path, unlike pickle
truncated before the actual signature-comparison logic; incomplete to judge either way
